title: Is the AI Hype Even Real?

modules.title: How AI Hype Gets Made and Who Benefits From It
modules.sub: Tracing the money and motives behind AI narratives
modules.description: You map out the ecosystem of players who produce and amplify AI hype, from startups seeking funding to media chasing clicks. By the end you can name the incentives behind a headline before you even finish reading it.

modules.title: What AI Is Actually Good At Right Now â Honestly
modules.sub: A grounded look at genuine AI strengths today
modules.description: You work through real, documented examples of tasks where current AI systems perform well and explore why those specific use cases click. This gives you an honest baseline so you can spot overclaiming the moment it appears.

modules.title: What AI Gets Wrong: Hallucinations, Bias, and Blind Spots
modules.sub: Understanding the failure modes that headlines ignore
modules.description: You examine concrete cases where AI systems confidently produced wrong, biased, or harmful outputs and analyze the structural reasons those failures happen. You leave with a mental checklist of red flags to apply whenever you encounter an AI output in the wild.

modules.title: Reading AI Headlines Without Getting Played
modules.sub: Decoding the language tricks journalists and PR use
modules.description: You practice dissecting real AI headlines to identify loaded language, missing context, and buried caveats that change the story entirely. You build a repeatable reading habit that slows you down just enough to catch manipulation before it sticks.

modules.title: The Business of AI Doom and AI Optimism
modules.sub: Who profits from fear and who profits from excitement
modules.description: You investigate how both extreme pessimism and extreme optimism about AI are commercially useful positions, and who holds them strategically. You decide for yourself where hype ends and legitimate forecasting begins by following the financial and reputational incentives.

modules.title: AI Risk: Separating Real Concerns From Noise
modules.sub: Distinguishing evidence-based risks from speculative panic
modules.description: You sort a curated set of AI risk claims into categories based on the quality of evidence and the timeframe involved, distinguishing near-term documented harms from long-range speculation. You walk away able to engage seriously with risk conversations without either dismissing or catastrophizing.

modules.title: Apply It: Audit an AI Claim You've Seen This Week
modules.sub: Putting every tool to work on a real claim
modules.description: You choose an AI claim you personally encountered, then run it through the full set of frameworks from earlier modules including source incentives, capability realism, and language analysis. You produce a short written audit you could share with someone else as a worked example.

modules.title: Building Your Ongoing AI Radar
modules.sub: Creating habits that keep your BS detector current
modules.description: You design a lightweight personal system for staying calibrated as AI capabilities and narratives keep evolving, including the sources you follow and the questions you ask first. You finish the course with a living document that is yours to update, not a snapshot that goes stale.

synopsis: Every week there's a new headline saying AI will either save the world or end it â and most of those headlines are selling you something. This course gives you the thinking tools to evaluate AI claims on your own so you're never the person repeating nonsense at dinner.
rationale: Young adults are the most exposed generation to AI misinformation and hype cycles, yet critical AI media literacy is almost entirely absent from accessible AI education.
learning_outcome: After this course, you can independently evaluate AI claims, identify who benefits from a given narrative, and explain the real capabilities and limits of current AI systems to others.
source_signals: Things are about to get worse for generative AI
Generative AI coding tools and agents do not work for me
the science and implications of generative ai
